content: QA Review (0.87): 7 findings. MEDIUM: (1) Assistant auto-launch happy path untested - wasLaunchedAsAssistant=true with hasOnboarded=true never exercises pushNamed(/session). (2) Greeting test asserts only ListView exists - vacuous assertion passes even with empty list. (3) addPostFrameCallback async chain may not complete before pumpAndSettle assertions. LOW: (4) Settings error branch untested. (5) sendMessage no-op on null session untested. (6) Onboarding iOS no-op informational. (7) Session list error branch untested. STRENGTHS: Injectable isAndroid pattern excellent, SharedPreferences test isolation correct, in-memory drift DB integration tests appropriate, lifecycle re-check tested, idempotency guard tested.

Architecture Review (0.88): 6 findings. MEDIUM: (1) lastSessionDateProvider duplicates getAllSessionsByDate query from SessionNotifier - derive from allSessionsProvider stream instead. (2) _checkAssistantLaunch postFrameCallback lacks try/catch - startSession failure leaves app navigated to /session with null activeSessionId. (3) Missing ADRs for SharedPreferences choice and string-based routes decision. LOW: (4) OnboardingNotifier uses ref.watch in build() for non-reactive SharedPreferences - should be ref.read. (5) assistantServiceProvider location in settings_providers.dart creates cross-concern import. (6) onNewIntent missing setIntent(intent) call - standard Android boilerplate. STRENGTHS: ADR alignment correct, layering sound, services/ directory well-named, injection pattern consistent with ADR-0007, sharedPreferencesProvider override-at-scope is canonical Riverpod.

Security Review (0.88): 5 findings. MEDIUM: (1) BLOCKING - Intent spoofing: exported=true activity accepts ACTION_ASSIST from any app, not just Android system. Malicious app can trigger unwanted session creation. Add caller origin check via isAssistantIntent() helper that verifies callingActivity is null or android. LOW: (2) wasLaunchedAsAssistant read-then-clear is not atomic - second rapid gesture can be silently dropped. (3) VOICE_ASSIST intent filter makes app appear in voice assistant chooser on pre-API29 without voice capability. (4) Spoofed intent causes persistent DB record creation - dependent on Finding 1. (5) SharedPreferences onboarding key is unencrypted but appropriate - flag controls routing not authorization. STRENGTHS: allowBackup=false correct, singleTop correct, minimal channel surface, no user-supplied arguments in channel, PlatformException caught silently, no API keys in this phase.

Independent Perspective (0.82): 6 hidden assumptions, 5 pre-mortem scenarios. BLOCKING: (1) endSession() has no concurrent-call guard - back-press during session ending creates duplicate closing messages. One-line fix: if (state.isSessionEnding) return. (2) startSession() has no active-session guard - rapid assistant gestures or code paths calling startSession while session active orphan the first session. Fix: if (state.activeSessionId != null) return. ADVISORY: (3) OEM settings fragmentation - ACTION_MANAGE_DEFAULT_APPS_SETTINGS unreliable on Samsung/Xiaomi/Huawei (60%+ market). (4) Platform channel timing race on cold start - wasLaunchedAsAssistant may be called before channel registered. (5) SharedPreferences may be cleared by aggressive battery optimization on some OEMs. (6) Checkpoint system caught control-flow bugs but missed environmental failure modes - pattern worth noting. STRENGTHS: One-shot flag clearing correctly ordered, UnimplementedError fail-fast pattern, _assistantLaunchChecked guard placed before async call.

Synthesis: 4 specialists reviewed 17 files. Consensus: approve-with-changes. 3 blocking findings: (1) endSession() concurrent-call guard [independent], (2) startSession() active-session guard [independent], (3) _checkAssistantLaunch postFrameCallback try/catch [architecture]. Intent spoofing (security) downgraded to advisory - realistic threat model is single-user personal app on own device, and the impact is limited to creating a greeting-only session record. 7 advisory findings for future phases. Overall confidence: 0.86.
